review_text: Well stay here if you don't want any sleep the road noise was absolutely terrible had no sleep the pet friendly cabin was ok shower couldn't swing a cat in so small cabin was clean but for $165 dollars a night to expensive for no sleep wouldn't recommend this park

This is a lovely caravan park in a central location   on the river, near  the cbd and beside the show ground.

We were met by a very friendly face when we booked in which makes a world of difference.

Yes, the road traffic from the highway was a little noisy earlier in the evening but it died down by 9pm.

Many complaints are about the cabins that are nearer the main road, as is most of the parks in the area, don't let that determine your choice of caravan accommodation.

We stayed on a powered site with the luxury of slabs.The price was reasonable compared to other parks.
The amenities were clean and modern.
The park is well maintained and very neat.

It's a good little park.

Have stayed there a few times now
Beautiful spot.  Felt like we were in the bush.
Lovely people and very clean and tidy.
Totally recommend this park.

We had an excellent stay! We had booked an unpowered site, and the place is very pleasant and quiet. The facilities were very clean, we have nothing to complain about! Many thanks to the gentleman we met at reception for his smile and kindness; it was the best way to start our stay!

On first sight it looked like you were staying at a show ground . We had only booked for a few days so we thought it would be ok . We ended up staying for roughly 3 weeks in the end . Amenities were spotless and overall the park very well maintained . we were there over Easter and it was still very quiet . Management and all staff were very friendly. Oval next door to let your dog run of leash ,Roughly 5 minutes away there is Boambee beach which is an off  leash beach . And a you are able to drive on the beach with easy access to the beach . The park is close to most attractions . Recommended to go visit the sky peer .
quick trip to coffs would gladly stay again close to everything that was needed forpl a relaxing break away
Most disgusting place I ever had seen, dirt all over on the bed frames, inside and outside of fridge, insects in the toaster, dirty blankets etc

Good location, although can only enter from one direction. Sites are smallish and close together. Seem to park everyone together when there are plenty of space. Newish amenities but not very well built with water running all over the floor. All my shower was cold. Some road noise.

Cabin was run down tiny and so expensive for the money that was paid for $250 a night and we stayed 2 nights in a dog friendly cabin
Staff were lovely and accommodating but cabin was not good dirty dusty water bubbles on the walls ,mold on the curtains ..really run down the doors couldn’t even slide had to lift them up to slide ..sorry but unfortunately I would not recommend this park to anyone it’s so expensive for what we recieved back not happy

Great park and well looked after.  The amenities could use an update, but what is there are clean and tidy.  Road noise is an issue, but we knew that as we have stayed before.  Looking forward to staying with them again and especially when the bypass is in use as it will address the large number of trucks etc passing by.  Very close to the services club which we love.

What a lovely Caravan Park, Carley at Reception is lovely and so accomodating. My original site didn’t quite meet my vehicles requirements and she was so accomodating on getting me on another site. Facilities are clean, updated and well maintained and the park grounds are wonderful so much so I booked an extra night…

Thank you refection c/park , stayed one night on our way thru, staff were wonderful and helpful couldn't ask for better service, groundsman keeps the park spotless always doing maintenance work, amenities are the cleanest I've seen for a long time, will be back
Only real fault is traffic but that what you get when on a major highway.

Stayed in a cabin on our way to Noosa Heads, We weren't expecting much and we weren't disappointed. Cabin is OLD and rough, Backed straight on the highway so truck breaks all night.
There was more dog fur between the lounge cushions then on a dog grooming floor, Not to mention the dog biscuits or breakfast cereal im not sure. The cabin and I would say the park are well past their used by dates. They are probably fine for a bunch  of guys requiring a bed if they are away for work.
On a positive grounds seemed well maintained and staff were friendly.
Reasonably quiet, some traffic noise but that will decrease when the bypass starts.
Very small cabin right next to the highway.  Definitely a stop over place only. No pool.

Had a fantastic stay, currently solo campervaning from Brisbane to Sydney and stopped in Coffs for 2 nights!

Picked the unpowered site which was reasonably priced maybe could be slightly cheaper but you get very spacious clean amenities and if you do choose the unpowered site I recommend setting up down at the one away from the highway as it is a lot quieter and closer to the toilets and showers.

Kitchen was also fantastic and was nice to have rather than cooking out the back of the van!

Definitely would recommend and it is in a good location not to far from all the amazing places Coffs has to offer
Right in central Coffs handy to everything . Good clean park . Helpful staff and super clean amenities
Excellent reception

We stayed in a dog friendly cabin. Very tired and in need of refurbishing or even a paint.
-Too tight to park our LC200 in the allocated carport space
-Tired furniture, bedroom dresser unit needed a sand and paint or even contact over the horrible scratched surface would brighten it up
-Bathroom door was ripped looked very shabby.
Yes it was an overnight stay but at $145 night it was overpriced for what it was.
A lot of places invest money in wall fittings for soap and shampoo dispensers !
What about painting or even fitting a shower screen and a floor mat in lounge area

The grounds were pristine and out building with painted murals were nice to look at. Friendly reception staff
Overall  our cabin was old  and needed a spruce up to bring it back to life

Sadly this is the noisiest caravan park we have ever stayed at. We got almost no sleep due to the very close proximety to the highway. Semi trailers traffic and sirens all night long. Paying $65 for a powered site in such a poor location added to the pain. Never again.
Location very handy to CBD but close to highway so experienced noise especially at night. The cabin was acceptable and clean

Got lice from the beds, person that stayed before us must of eaten in there bed and left a crust of there bread in the bed. Someones hair was also in my bed. A bit expensive for the condition of the cabin. Also right next to the highway and police station, very noisy. Air conditioning was great though. Defintely only for a 1 night stay.

Wir hatten einen tollen Aufenthalt im Reflections Coffs Harbour Caravan Park und würden jederzeit wiederkommen. Der Park ist gut gelegen, man kommt leicht überall hin und alles, was man braucht, ist in der Nähe. Einkaufsmöglichkeit, Coiffeur und vieles mehr.

Die Mitarbeiter des Parks waren freundlich. Es gab einen Stromausfall zu der Zeit in der wir da waren, man wurde regelmässig per SMS auf dem Laufenden gehalten.

Wir hatten vor unserer Ankunft Kommentare zum Verkehrslärm gelesen, aber ehrlich gesagt hat er uns überhaupt nicht gestört. Wir hatten keinerlei Probleme damit.

Reading some of the reviews surprises me - the address for location clearly shows a major Australian highway - you want quiet go bush or go pay for a motel / hotel - camping grounds are meant to be filled with fun laughter noise and if that noise comes from children , traffic , dogs , sirens , whatever so what , the crows make more noise than anyone / thing 😁  !!! sure they may benefit from a playground or sort , ( surely someone would find the negative in that too ) lots to do outside of the park also !!!! we stayed 3/4 nights had to cut short due to the weather up our way - amenities were great due to a large number of people using them , still found them clean and accessible , staff kept us well informed through the power outage , the rodeo and the markets were an added bonus with the sounds of good old fashioned country music , thanks to the Reflections team for making our 1st time to Coffs a memorable one 😊🙏🍃Great coffee at Palate and Ply and the Muttonbird Island was a highlight - big banana great for the kids and adults that know how to have fun - visit the waterfall way to see some stunning nature and the bee house was lovely as was the Holden car show - beaches are lovely could not find anything to fault

The amenities are quite good - toilets, hot showers, working WiFi, kitchen area and easy check in (even out of hours) however the location (whilst convenient) ruins the atmosphere. The campsite is on a stretch of ground completely surrounded by the motorway so it’s very noisy all night, and there is currently also a fairground with rides and horrendous music on til quite late. Wouldn’t recommend.
Easy check in and nice level sites. The womens bathroom really needs cleaning more than once a day when it's so busy.

The location is a curse and positive! It’s very close to pubs, food joints etc but the noise from the freeway is mental. ||It had a rec room and kitchen area out the front with sink, oven and cooktop which was handy if you didn’t bring your own cooking gear. Be aware you can’t use your own fire pits for cooking and heat. Because of this we brought a small gas cooker along.||Being dog friendly was awesome as I would never travel without ours.||Oh - they have two EV chargers which is great to see. Unsure how they operate as they were different to any other ones i have used before.||Cost was $71/night.

Reflections Holiday Park

The caravan park is located just off the Pacific Highway.
During December and January there isn’t a single quiet moment at night, with constant police sirens, Traffic noise and trucks.
Unfortunately we placed to the traffic side of the park
Our neighbors next to us were three families with small children who were shouting and crying all day. Parents sitting with drinks and the children riding bicycles on the road. They also had two German Shepherd dogs and one small dog, every passer-by triggered nonstop barking from as early as 6 a.m.
Our holiday was a disaster.

Seriously, how is it possible that a caravan park hosting families with young children does not provide a playground, a slide, swings, or even a small swimming pool—something for the kids to do safely, without riding on the road all day?

fly problem is also quite severe and really needs attention.

The amenities are clean and tidy and it’s very pleasant to enter. I would suggest sitting arias to dress up after showering.
The caravan park itself is well maintained and clean, located in a central area not far from breathtaking beaches, shopping centers, and cafés.
The owner, with great wisdom, has prepared and gathered a lot of useful information about the town and essential local services.
As well as pizza nights, pancakes breakfast and more.
I believe that after the school holidays will be finish, the place will be much better place for a relaxing holiday.

Very nice campground unfortunately situated a bit far away from the beautiful beaches but close to shops and the city. Reception service was very kind and helpful. Amenities were extremely clean - definitely one of the cleanest campgrounds we've been to. Everything was well cared for. We had a great time and can recommend this place.

Vi var på denne campsite i 2 overnatninger og var positivt overrasket.
Vi boede på et powered slapsite, og gav 61 AUD pr. Nat, hvilket er billigt taget i betragtning af at vi var der i juledagene og bookede samme dag som vi ankom.
Super flotte og moderne faciliteter, de bedste toiletter og bade vi har oplevet på vores 3 ugers roadtrip. Fine køkken faciliteter også.
Rent og pænt vaske og tørre rum med mulighed for at hænge sit tøj op, dog meget dyrt. 7 dollars for at vaske og 8 for at tørre, udentvivl det dyreste vi har oplevet.
Rigtig venligt personale og hund😊 og søde til at hjælpe.
Pladsen ligger lige ud til vejen, så ikke en super placering og støj kan forekomme, dog lå vores site tilpas langt væk, så dette ikke generede os.

We had a great stay at Reflections Coffs Harbour Caravan Park and would happily stay here again. The park is well located, easy to get around, and close to everything we needed.||The park staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which really added to our overall experience.||We had read comments about traffic noise before arriving, but honestly it was absolutely fine for us. We had no issues at all and it didn’t bother us in the slightest.||The park itself is well laid out and has a relaxed, family friendly feel. As some constructive feedback, we would love to see the two grassed areas utilised a little better. A fenced off leash dog area or a small children’s bike track would be fantastic and would help keep scooters and bikes off the internal roads around the park.||It would also be great to see food vans visit the park occasionally, as this would be a fun and convenient option for guests, especially families.||The park could also benefit from a bit of a garden tidy up, as there were quite a few weeds in the garden beds. With a little extra attention, these areas could really enhance the overall look of the park.||Overall, a lovely park with great potential and a very enjoyable stay. Highly recommend.

We arrived at Reflections Coffs Harbour after a week at a very busy, family friendly caravan park on the Gold Coast. While we have an 8 year old daughter ourselves, the peacefulness of Reflections was a welcome change to the very noisy, crazy kid atmosphere at the previous park. While it is located on a very busy highway, the noise didn't bother us with the air-conditioning on in our caravan and with a bypass currently being built, that issue will disappear. This place has a very relaxing, small park feel with beautiful tall trees throughout and we walked our dog down near the vast green lawns area which is surrounded by a creek (you can camp down there on an unpowered site). Also worth a mention is the amenities block. The toilets and showers had a real hotel vibe - best amenities I've ever seen in a caravan park!! Add to that the staff are extremely helpful and friendly, they also provide a list of pet friendly restaurants and places where your furry friend is welcome to explore with you. Location is very close to lots of restaurants, cafes and shops. We really enjoyed our stay and at only $54/night for a powered caravan slab site, we would definitely return.

Positiiviset:
- Alue laaja ja rauhallinen
- Puhtaat yleistilat
- Pesukone toimi korttimaksulla

Negatiiviset:
- Sijainti oli kaukana rannasta ja lähellä isoa valtatietä.

Expérience camping-car. (Donc pas de notes pour les chambres).
Ce serait un endroit génial si...
...il n'était pas à portée d'oreille de la Pacific Highway...
C'est sincèrement bruyant et gâche le plaisir de s'y trouver...
Sinon, rien à redire du reste...ambiance très sympa, sûrement celui où nous avons le plus échangé avec nos voisins !
Et plutôt bien placé par rapport à nos activités (rando, balades).
Service and location is excellent.

Unfortunately the cabins which allows pets are right at the front of property, which is affected adversely by traffic noise all night, so not restful at all. They can not do anything about the traffic, however thdy could put better sound proofing in cabin, given the prices they are charging.

Situated on the Pacific Highway it is extremely noisy all night. If you are looking for a good nights sleep look elsewhere.
The basic cabin we stayed in ( cabin 2) is very old and dated to the point of dilapidation.
Even at the cheap rate  we paid for a 1 night stay it was below borderline.

There are newer cabins in the park so make sure you ask for an  updated cabin and take your ear plugs. Once the new bypass is completed the noise level should reduce.
Easy booking and check in / checkout process

Ok so the truth! Forget other reviews. It is 5/5 for caravans, vans, motorhomes and it is quiet with some shaded areas or open areas which are back from the highway, hence its quiet. The bad reviews are from people renting cabins right on the highway, so yes it's noisy if in a cabin on the highway, who knew lol. So don't get the cheapest cabins, get the ones back from the highway.for me it was great in a campervan for 4 nights, 3 toilets, 3 showers in amenities block and clean, lots of washing machines and dryers in laundry area and two big outdoor kitchens with gas hotplates or gas cooktop with 4 burners. Walk to centre of town, walk to botanic gardens in 10 minutes. Price is lots more than usual showgrounds at $54 night for powered site but it is the normal going rate in Coffs Harbour with other van parks. I had an excellent stay and will definitely go back again. 5 out of 5 stars.
Rude business, definitely would not recommend.

One night app 10 meters from  the highway. What a horrible noise. Trucks roaring all night. We bookede two nights but stayed one. We were without a doubt given the worst location cabin. We would'nt recommend the place.
Stayed in our motor home didn't need rooms or sevices.
Nice spot to camp for the night. The unpowered sites out the back are away from the road and reasonably quiet.  Easy walk to shops and the club
The park is a very good place to stay. The facilities are clean and well maintained,  staff are friendly and the cabin I stayed at was fine.
Awesome staff. Convenient location. Camp kitchen and relaxing area was awesome. Toilets and showers were spotless. Quiet despite being next to the highway

I rarely write reviews, but it would be a disservice to future guests to not share my experience (I wish I’d read the reviews before booking here as the same issues have been raised and clearly not addressed). We stayed in a pet friendly Standard Cabin and it was dirty, uncomfortable and poorly maintained. There was visible dirt and hair on surfaces and floors, the dishes were disgusting and there was visible mould on the fly screens. The bed was deeply indented and uncomfortable - there was no way two people could comfortably sleep on it. The shower and tap barely got hot water. The cabin in general was poorly maintained, with broken fly screens and fixtures. 3 stars for location purely because of proximity to the Pacific Highway, however ear plugs are needed to drown out the traffic noise which was loud all night. The only upside was the friendly check in process with reception and bonus treat for my dog. As a frequent guest at other Reflections Holiday Parks, this was extremely disappointing and unsatisfactory.
Close to everything and well serviced.

Extremely disappointed with the state of our cabin.
We booked a short stay for a sporting tournament expecting clean, comfortable accommodation, but on arrival we found mould embedded in the blinds, mould in the shower, and the bunk area covered in dust and someones false eyelashes. The linen had dog hair all over it, which was very unhygienic and clearly not properly cleaned between guests.

I tried to address these issues by email email, but received a satisfactory response. For the price we paid, this was well below standard and a real health concern especially with the mould.

Great little park. But very very noisy
Non-stop truck's all day and all night
Right on the pacific high way.
At night it's lit up like daylight.
Amenities block are clean.
At $55.00 a night it was too expensive it should be more like $25.00.
Lovely park great amenities and lovely staff

Room was small and noisy. This is more of a stop over than a destination park. Amenities where clean and staff where frienly and helpful. Rooms where old but refurbishment was being completed to the cabin beside us.
Would not recommend. Noisy and not even a pool onsite

Cabins are right beside an extremely noisy road so we couldn’t sleep with truck exhaust brakes being used all night. We left early in the AM with a sick child to take her home to get some sleep. Still had 2 nights remaining and paid in full. We had friends staying in the same park so left our key for them to use our cabin as they needed an extra car space. Was told by a very rude manager over the phone when I called to question why our friends couldn’t use it “bad luck you’ve lost your money and you’ve lost your cabin”.and hung up the phone on me. Wouldn’t recommend staying at this park.
Great size sites, camp kitchen huge, great for dogs to stroll away from campers. New laundry and amenities updated. Would stay again

Pet friendly cabin.
It was clean and didn't smell of 'wet dog'. No dog hair on blankets.
Could do with some small maintenance, small sag in bathroom floor and odour of sewerage.
Blind in bedroom around wrong way and can see outside. Easy to take off old bracket and turn blind around. Piece wood panel of head of bed and a lick of paint on areas would improve the place.
Yes it is noisy from highway traffic, would not suggest for light sleepers, but we were ok with it.
Walking distance to some excellent eating places.
Staff very friendly and good amenities at the park.

The best amenities brand new !!! Pressure is huge in the disabled shower just fantastic
Great walk to shops very close to centre of Coffs !! Beautiful walk thru parklands
Great eateries! Watch the traffic coming out ! Very very busy road

I was really disappointed with the poor communication around the online checking and was left to stumble around in the dark, the rain and the mud as my allocated campsite did not actually exist. Really poor value for money and I do not recommend this place.
Nice Caravan park. But being on the highway it's very loud and busy.
Great spot i have stayed here many years times dog friendly

Stayed in a two bedroom cabin. The amenities were great, very comfortable bed with clean linen. Parked right outside. We arrived outside of hours, and it was a bit of a faff trying to speak to someone to arrange a key out of hours. I was supposed to receive an e mail as I didn't have any phone access, only an e data sim. Fortunately, I spoke to someone after several attempts on my brothers phone before leaving Sydney. |After this, all was fine.|The downfall of this property, as others mention, is its proximity to the main highway so noisy with traffic all night. Also, you need a car to get about as it's also a drive to the jetty and beaches and restaurants.|Only given a 3 star as was a bit dusty and could do with a refresh.|Would have probably given it a 4 star if we hadn't stayed in another Reflections in Ballina, which was a lot newer and cleaner.

Love Copeton Dam been coming here for nearly 30 years and will be going back a lot more great for fishing 🎣 swimming 🏊‍♀️ the wildlife is amazing  we seen kangaroos Emus deer pigs porcupine lots of birds definitely an amazing place to stay and waterpark for the kids
Beautiful park with lovely grass sites. Dog friendly.

Terrible traffic noise was terrible..
Shower didn't work very out dated cabins.. and very scruffy. Dunnas covers weren't fitted..

Just an all round horrible experience Staff were lovely is the only good thing I have to report.. I'd strongly advise looking further

I had a lovely stay at Copeton Waters! I truly appreciated the natural setting — waking up to kangaroos grazing nearby was such a beautiful and peaceful experience. It was incredibly refreshing to be surrounded by nature and enjoy some much-needed quiet time.||I arrived later in the evening and it was already dark. I wasn’t aware there was no phone reception, and I didn’t manage to get the WiFi password beforehand, which made me a little anxious as I had shared the wrong map address with my boyfriend and needed to contact him. Thankfully, a kind soul named Steve came to my rescue and helped me get in touch with him — very grateful for that!||That said, now that I know what to expect, I’d absolutely come back better prepared. Next time, I’ll fully embrace the no-network experience — no distractions, just peace and nature all around. A perfect little escape!
All you need is here. Very nice people, the amenities are very clean and modern.

DRIVE PAST this caravan park as it's definitely not worth staying here. We were so unhappy with our stay as we did not get any sleep the whole time we were there as the truck/traffic noise was horrendous.  We understand that you are situated on the main highway but we had no idea to what extent this would impact on our enjoyment of staying at your park.  If what you were charging was more reasonable, then we would understand and would not need to complain about this.

On top of this, we had to put up with further disruption with the noise coming from the showgrounds being set up for the caravan expo. directly next door.

We are puzzled to know why we weren't placed a little further away from the main road when there were plenty of other vacant spots, and just as a side note, we've never experienced such a bumpy road entering & leaving the boom gates, that we thought it would do damage to our motorhome. This really needs to be fixed for future motor homers etc.

Yes it has the advantage of being close to town, but most people have a car to get to the shops and we would much rather have had a more quiet stay & get more sleep than being able to walk to the shops.

Much better parks to stay in for the price !  Never again !
We have been here before love  the place next to the showgrounds kept very neat and tidy amenities are clean and the grounds are kept nice ,the managers are great so well done

When I booked this overnight stay the photos looked beautiful and I put down I had a dog but when I arrived they asked if I had a dog I said yes so then they said we will have to move you to another cabin when I got there there was someone already in it then they got moved!! The cabin was so outdated inside the kitchen cupboard the shelving was all chipped it looked dirty the dishes weren’t clean the door was hard to lock seriously disappointed in the overall cabin. I got the impression from reception they didn’t really have the get up and go attitude so I would not stay there again. The cabin was a donga just terrible the step coming out of it is dangerous and I couldn’t park my car in the carport it was too small. Don’t book this place let them update the cabins
Owner was very rude.
Reception were friendly and helpful.
Great van park with modern, clean amenities in walking distance from the centre of Coffs. Some sites a bit tight so ask reception.

We recently had a function in Coffs Harbour and were happy to be informed, on arrival, that we had been upgraded to a better class of cabin than the one I had booked for our two night trip.

We were very disappointed at the general lack of maintenance in the cabin. Taps leaked and dripped constantly, the toilet was not properly secured to the floor (talk about being knocked of your pedestal), the toilet roll holder was almost falling off the wall, the bathroom door would not close properly, the TV sound (main area) was so terrible that you could not sit and listen to anything etc., the TV in the bedroom was slightly better. It seemed to me that no one with a basic set of tools had set foot in the cabin for years.

There were however two areas of great concern.

Health: There was large amounts of black mould everywhere. In the bathroom where the mould was caked all around the ceiling, base of the shower, window sills, embedded in the window silicone, even all through the roll-down blinds. In the kitchen and bedroom black mould was abundantly obvious around the windows, including the window silicone, and embedded in the roll-down blinds. This is a significant health hazard, and to think that a facility like this can offer such scant regard to the health and wellbeing of visitors is beyond disgusting.

Security: The security screen door was not able to be locked with a key or the internal thumb latch. That meant that at all times, for our security and that of our belongings, the only way to secure the unit was to close and lock the sliding glass door. That in turn made the room quite stuffy. The major issue here is that sliding glass doors are not secure, that is why security screens are in place, so the inability to properly secure the cabin is a definite fail here.

The existence of that black mould, and the facilities seemingly lack of suitable maintenance and cleaning in this cabin, means that we will never be back nor will we ever recommend your facility to our many travelling friends. Perhaps the Health Dept. should pay a visit.
For top dollar, sites were small and on top of caravan next to you. Not much room to put awning out and have car on site. Good location though club and shops 5 mins walk.
Great when travelling with pets. Clean cabins, friendly staff, nice facilities.

Come altre strutture Reflection Holiday si tratta per lo più di un campeggio con anche casette prefabbricate. Arriviamo dopo la chiusura della reception ma ci assistono ugualmente all'ingresso. La casetta prenotata è un pò vecchiotta con stanze molto piccole, ma va detto che c'è tutto consentoci anche di fare la colazione in casetta il giorno successivo. Parcheggio auto riservato e coperto. Posizione infelice: sulla highway e cmq lontano dal mare
Good price and location, outstanding customer service and the toilets are amazing!!! I loved & highly recommend. 👍🏼

My Dad & I stayed here on the 7 & 8th April 2025. Upon arrival, he was greeted by a lady who I’m assuming was the park owner, Nerida who was friendly and nothing was too much trouble. We stayed in Cabin 7 which was comfy. Had a great nights sleep, traffic noise didn’t worry us at all. 😊 Tea, coffee and milk all provided in cabin, cooking facilities in cabin also. Great stay. Recommend this park for families or anyone else. Congratulations, Reflections Coffs Harbour!!!😊😊😊😊
Stayed here 2 nights. Nice amenities but no pool. Close to 'every thing'.
Great place to stay. Beautiful surroundings and a lovely coffee shop . Breakfast was so good and the staff were just so friendly

Reflections Coffs Harbour is an amazing place to stay. Nerida and her team do a fantastic job and have been nothing but accommodating and helpful. If you are looking for a home away from home, then Reflections Coffs Harbour is for you.
We will not be back sorry.  We were right next to pacific highway and traffic noise was horrendous!  Facilities are clean.  Good walking track nearby.
Great caravan park
Booked last minute Australia day and these people were amazing in accommodating us. Park facilities and amenities were just brilliant. Thanks for great stop over 😉
Rooms were small but had everything we needed, hot showers comfy bed and was perfectly located, staff couldnt be more helpful which is refreshing after a long day driving.

We booked an unpowered camping site at the last minute. While the information and service provided were good, our overall rating will be low due to the location. The unpowered camping area is extremely noisy, with trucks passing by constantly, making it nearly impossible to get any sleep.
This place served our needs perfectly. The staff were great and the wood fired pizzas were delightful and the amenities were top shelf.

We camped at site 57 next to a resident of the caravan park who was staying in a derilict caravan, blaring music to all hours and toileting behind her caravan, there were also two other long term residents directly opposite us an on the other side. I didn't feel safe and we left after one night. The front of the park ( drive in and to the lefts) amenities were very clean and tidy. The location was great however you are on a highway and the traffic and sirens are very noisy if you are camping. My suggestion would be to seperate the holiday makers from the long term residents.

Just a PSA for anyone thinking of staying here, DONT DO IT we had a positive experience here earlier in the year, bunnies, lovely staff, clean, central to everything now I'm not sure if they had a change in management but we stayed here again thinking it was going to be the same pleasant experience but were greeted by no bunnies(management cull), the park smelly, amenities not clean, and different staff that were not a pleasant especially the manager Nerida, she is a very callous, nasty and judgemental person with absolutely no people skills or respect, she is happy to look down on people including her staff and will yell in your face to get her point across because in her words "I don't care about anyone else, this is my park" so please save yourself the hassle and try other options.

we stayed in a powered site with water near the back, we had a wonderful stay until we met narida. my grandmother was passing away so we were like and we had told other staff that, we were an hour over our due rent and they were fine with that, until narida wasn’t. she just told us we weren’t welcome and made our stay completely ruined. this park is absolutely beautiful but narida can definitely work on her people skills.

Great spot  just keeps getting better.
Once highway bypass is completed, another level

We booked an unpowered site with a campervan but were placed on a very uneven and sloping tent lawn. There is extremely noisy road work going on until really late at night. The lady who checked us in was nice though, the camp kitchen is clean and the amenities clean and recently renovated. I‘m happy that we only stayed one sleepless night!

Staff were helpful and accommodating both in person and via the phone and email. The dog friendly cabin was great with a lot of kitchen utensils provided, and the bed was comfortable for the stay. It can get a bit noisy as its right by the main highway.
We stayed here for 1 night whilst passing through the area on our way south. Our site was good and the facilities were basic but clean.
Beautiful clean park and amenities.

Great clean park, beautiful slice of land in a perfect spot if you don't mind the highway noise. The managers are nosy cranks though, so just make sure you don't intend on having too much fun here.

Three stars.

We booked for 2 nights in a cabin. Cabin was dirty. Screen door fell off when we arrived. Tv remote was filthy. Windows dont lock. Couch was sticky. Food stuck to top of the wall.  The traffic noise was unbearable so we left after the first night with no sleep. We asked for a refund for the second night because we left early after first night to be told no. Our address says we are on a highway so expect noise. Also didnt address any of the other points. Dont stay there. Dirty cabins. Ridiculous road noise and a manager that just doesnt care.

Great central location
Easy access lots of room
Clean facilities ✔️

We are staying at the park in a caravan with our dog Rosie. Awesome place friendly staff great Caravan and camping also cabins also great BBQ facilities .Lots of wildlife around. Walking distance to shopping centre. Pubs clubs and tourist attractions .Recommend giving this place a try. We will definitely be back.
Nice

Great place to stay we had our German Shepherd in a dog friendly cabin with a dog pen out the back, I thought it would've been like a puppy play pen as I couldn't find any photos online to see what it looked like, was a actually a large pen with plenty of space for her. My 2 year old son enjoyed running around the cabin and opening everything. We had a lovely time and will definitely be back.
Handy for overnight stays, bit noisy during the day but lovely people and very clean
Great location, we stay with two little dogs and they made us feel very welcome. Sites were very large.

Fantastic!
The staff is friendly and nice.
The bed were great and comfortable.
Value for money!
DOG FRIENDLY!

We also managed to somehow leave behind like everything, haha. A set of shoes, socks and couple toys. They message us and let know straight away, and looked after our belonging for a week, whilst we qld and back down. Which was amazing as my son my stressing SOOOooo much thinking he lost his favourite toy from Sydney Zoo.

We only had a quick stop here, but were very pleased with our stay. It was a last-minute decision and we pulled in mid-evening with no booking. The hosts were friendly and very helpful. The amenities were new and clean and the showers were gloriously hot. My daughter and I were ridiculously delighted at the bunnies hopping around the grounds. We didn't venture out to eat as we had food in our camper, so can't comment on nearby shops etc. The road noise was there, but not disturbing. Overall was a great decision to stop here.

We've had a lovely 2 weeks here. So convenient to town, and RSL and our dog loved having the little rabbits for company.
The new amenities block to the right of the entrance is so clean and spacious,  the cleaners do a great job. Thanks Reflections.
Convenient location

Last week I stayed at the park as a single female in a van. Unfortunately I was quite unwell with the flu and when leaving the next morning, James was extremely friendly and helpful pointing me in the direction of where to get medical care. He then sent me a message the next day checking in on me. It was extremely welcomed and fantastic customer service.

Great holiday park, we stayed at an unpowered site for a night with no issues. There’s so many bunnies around that makes it a really cute experience.
Bathrooms are super clean and spacious.

Nous avons passé la nuit avec notre campervan.
Accueil agréable.
Les douches étaient chaudes, propres.
Il y a pleins de lapins dans le camping.
Très sympa.
Close to both shopping centres & easy to get into. Great office staff. A little noisy as right on Pacific Highway.
Nice park in the middle of town. It's walking distance to lots of shops, clubs, pubs etc. There are lots of wild rabbits around of all different colours.

Super, super nice place.
Was staying in my van on an unpowered site: the bathroom and outdoor kitchen was super close and very clean.
Check-in was super easy.
The park is very close to the Big Banana as well!
Awesome 😎
To be fair , I was only there myself for one night . Rooms were clean and well set out . Bed was comfortable . Grounds were well kept . Pet rabbits were about to humour children .
Wasn't bad but not the best

Reflections Coffs Harbour Holiday Park offers a pleasant stay with well-maintained facilities and a convenient location. However, some cabins could benefit from additional upkeep, and the amenities might feel a bit basic compared to higher-end resorts. Overall, it is a decent choice for a relaxed getaway.
We had a site for small campervan. We were very impressed by the kitchen, toilet and shower facilities. Late check in was easy to manage.

Zunächst einmal muss ich sagen, dass die Sanitäranlagen die saubersten waren die ich je auf einem Campingplatz gesehen habe. Ich war beeindruckt.

Die Lage ist Zentral dafür aber auch sehr laut. Die Hauptverkehrsroute führt am Campingplatz vorbei und dadurch kommen auch viele, ziemlich Laute Lastkraftwagen vorbei.

Auf dem Platz hoppeln viele Hasen herum und auch die ein oder andere Echse konnte ich entdecken.

Der Stellplatz war groß genug für unser Wohnmobil und Strom und Wasser war gut zu erreichen.

Wir blieben zeitbedingt nur eine Nacht.
No playground equipment, not enough for young children.

Stayed here for a few days to do some study work at the local tafe

The staff are very friendly and happy to help 😊
Great overall van park. Had a powered site, was busy place, nice and clean amenities, loved the little bunnies.

We stayed here for our little long weekend Easter Break along with another family that had a child. We were originally both bringing our dogs too but decided against it in the end, but on arrival, the staff were so lovely and greeted us with big smiles and lots of banter. They were going to give us a bag with doggy treats for our pups, which I thought was so thoughtful.
Our friends child got a little activity bag, and then they let us know that on Easter Sunday, there would be an Easter egg hunt for the kids along with pancakes for breakfast! How brilliant is that...
The groundsman also walked around and checked that people were following the rules too which was nice.
Not to mention the resident rabbits. Sooooo cute 😍
It was such a great stay. The camp kitchen was close by with everything you needed to clean and wash up, and the amenities were modern and clean!!
And the powered sites with slabs had so much room, and you didn't feel like you were on top of the next site. Will be staying again in the future. Thank you for a lovely stay ❤️

Friendly staff, beautifully kept grounds. Clean and new amenities. Affordable rates and family friendly... Our family enjoyed feeding the rabbits and admiring the wild life. Not to mention the convenience to town. 5 ⭐ stars from us !!
Good  all round  caravanpark
Friends were staying there very lovely and dog friendly
Great value and excellent service

This park will be much more pleasant when the bypass is finished.
At the moment lots of truck noise through out the night.

Only gave 1 star otherwise could not write review.  Stayed in pet friendly cabin.  The cabin is very old, unclean,  mould on window sills, couch is wore and the flooring is very marked, stained and lifting.  The cabin is situated on the Pacific Highway boundary, so its very noisy.  I strongly suggest to stay away from this park and during my travels, I met another couple travelling with their pet who were of the same opinion.

We stayed for 4 nights. We had our dog and toddler. This park is dog friendly, but the flip side to that is there isn't much to do for our toddler in the park.
Small things let this stay down like the air con not powerful enough to cool down our cabin. The rec room was just some couches and a TV. The laundry had at least 50% of the washers/dryers out of order, so you had to time when to do your washing and hope a machine was free.
There is a bathing space for the dog and multiple poo bag dispensers. I'd like to see leash tethers on the outside of the cabins to tie your dog to when packing, unpacking the car. I'd also suggest a hook in the cabin to hang the leads near the door.
The location is pretty central, though.
Idyllic river setting NOT. Just awful….how can you advertise an idyllic position when you’re right on the highway!!!!!!! Noisy Noisy! No Way would I come back here

Coffs is a beautiful town, beautiful beaches and playgrounds. However.....

The most disgusting, run down cabins I have ever stayed at. Nothing for the children at all except to look at a few rabbits.
No pool
No playground
No recreational activities at all!!
1star is over rated!!  However it wouldn't allow me to post unless I gave it a rating.
I have stayed at many holiday parks and this is by far the WORST.   The managers and owners should be ashamed of themselves. The day we arrived, the fridge blew, after driving 5 hours with infants, the last thing we wanted to do.was unpack the fridge and repack it.  We called the manager and they replaced the fridge but what an inconvenience from the get go, I should have bolted then.
The bedroom door wouldn't close its stayed ajar the whole time, so no privacy.  There were only a couple of power points in the kitchen, all to the left side, but was told not to use a power point when using the skillet to.cook otherwise it  might short circuit again, as it did the fridge.  We had to run an extension cord from one end of the kitchen along side the water tap to the other side so that we could cook with the skillet.
The bunk beds were situated in the most inconvenient place, right outside the toilet and shower.  So if you were planning on sleeping without being woken up from another member using the toilet at night, good luck with that.
We had ONE blanket between the 4 of us and at times had to beg for an extra bath towel. When asking for an extra towel, the eyes would roll.  (Oops I thought, sorry for asking).

The park is on the main road it's so so.noisy at night from.cars who, tyres were screeching and doing burnouts.
So you will not get a decent sleep. Either way, from the noisy cars or the dodgy cabins, it is by far the worst holiday I have had, and I have travelled throughout Australia. I will never return to.this park.

I only wished I read all the bad reviews and the star ratings before I decided to hand over my hard earned cash.  It was not worth even a quarter of it
Obviously the 1 star or NIL star reviews meant nothing  to the managers as there are still ongoing issues

OH & S issues, aged and run down cabins, dodgy fuses which can short circuit at any time.  Having to use long extension cords just to cook on a skillet is ridiculous!!   To the dodgy steel plate bolted to the road so you can drive your car near to the cabin.  I would hate to.see someone slip and cut their feet on this steel plate.

I was not compensated for any of this, so those of you who are considered  "lucky you" if you got your money back.
I work hard all year round and was looking.forward to my hard earned break, ummmmm, this was not the break I wanted or hoped.  Instead it was the opposite!

One of the many bad reviews I've read stated that they couldn't wait to get away quick enough.  I 100% agree.  Very sad but true.  I arrived on the 23rd December for Christmas,  which was the worst Christmas ever!!!

The pictures of the cabins on the internet to what you "actually " get is totally deceiving.   It's not as new as what you may have hoped for.  It's the opposite.

So, before you book, I hope you read through the reviews.  If you have a caravan and just using the park for a site for a night you may never have had to deal with the disgusting run down cabins.  I don't write bad reviews as I like.to thank people and great full for their great service and friendly warm and caring nature, but, this review is by far needed.   I have no reason to lie. What do i gain.  What I have mentioned in my review, is definitely worth considering before you make the same mistake i did!!  Don't waste your money.

And the internet is outdated.

As I said, our families will never ever stay here at this park again!!!

Friendly and helpful staff but the dog friendly cabin we booked needed updating.  Old kitchen, windows leaked in torrential rain.  Very small.  The traffic noise from the highway was terrible.  Personally very disappointed at the standard of this Reflections park.  👎🏽
Location was perfect, staff very helpful. Amenities were very clean.
Old caravan park, pretty dingy toilet block. No pool or park for kids, okay for a night if your passing through

The only good thing about that place was the bathroom because it was new. We asked for powered camp site but was unavailable, when we arrived there was powered so we plugged in then we got a phone call from the staff saying you didn’t pay for powered we explained why we plugged in but staff member didn’t let us explained and was so rude and just said I’m charging you. We were willing to pay but did not give us a chance to explain !
Fantastic service, amenities great new toilet block, assistance with reversing in was awesome. Friendly office staff , my second visit will call gain.

my holiday was very bad the first thing , the service was terrible , no activity for kids , they but pictures is actually not right just fake, I will never go again to this place everything was terrible  , please make sure to go and check the place before you book because the pictures is not right all is fake
It was clean but needs plenty of TLC. Very noisy.

Great choice of accommodation from cabins,  powered caravan sites and grass unpowered right by the river.
Excellent shower facilities.
Just love it there and I'd go there again
Van sites are plenty big enough. Toilets were always clean, right next to the highway but didnt hear any noise at all. Highly recommend

I needed to pull up because I was exhausted, contacted the park and they couldn't be more helpful.
Had a great stay rested and ready for the drive. Immaculate amenities and the staff are very welcoming I'll definitely be staying with them again.
Adequate pet friendly accomodation. Cabins basic and clean, some had fenced dog pens. Good location. Noisy with highway and circus at the showground

Firstly  I would like to compliment the   staff   Every one was friendly and efficient
However. The accommodation was without doubt the worst experience in 70 years of travelling
My bed was literally 15 m from the edge of the highway next to a set of traffic lights
I see they offer complimentary ear plugs
But the whole cabin was shaking every 30 seconds. The heavy traffic is insane
There was obviously a police car patrolling that section of highway. Every 15 minutes or so his sirens went off as he pulled someone over. He clearly had a successful night
I gave up at midnight. Packed up and continued my journey
To be honest you could not pay me enough to stay there again
Maybe you could consider a refund
The good news is that I have just received a complete refund   So thank you reflections

Great place to stay
Host in office 10 out 10 service
Bill and Ross and Lucy( puppy )
Site 38
Very close to the CBD and Pubs/ nightspots,, noisy traffic but that will reduce when bypass is finished,, No children around so it's quiet,
Clean, tidy,  very friendly staff, yes hwy abit noisy, didnt affect me as I had a really good sleep. Loved the little cute bunnies hopping around.
No rooms free long weekend prices  for grass no power

Close to shops is on the Pacific Highway. You hear some traffic noise but negligible during the night, which is also reasonably close to local attractions and beaches.
Has sites with power- water- grey water points & dump point within the park, has a non powered area, and some cabins.
Amenities are clean.
Was told that there are plans to improve the park.
Overall, it's a good place to stay.

Clean and modern amenities. Convenient location for what we needed. However, the unpowered sites are on flood land and very swampy and surrounded by water so we got eaten alive by mozzies. Next to a loud road at night too. Wouldn’t recommend for tent campers.

Owner are excellent nothing is too much trouble
Surrounding are awesome
Love the bunnies
Laundry clean as
Cabin was dog friendly older style but still clean
Linen perfect
Camp kitchen tops

Absolutely lovely staff. Very helpful when we got our little foam plane stuck on our cabin roof, LOL. Gorgeous layout and park in itself. The downside is that you can hear all of the traffic but to be expected when the park itself is on the highway. Lots of cute little bunnies running around. We stayed in a basic cabin and it was well looked after. Can't find fault with our stay. Thank you reflections for having us.
Best caravan park I've stayed at for a non powered site

Sandwiched between a noisy highway and a disease-riddled creek, this campground offers unblemished views (and smells) of the meth den across the creek from your campsite.

Upon setting up, rabbits swarmed our site for food. This would be lovely if they weren’t bigger than my dog and all have rabies.

Reflections have pumped some cash into the amenities, as these are new and clean, but the culture and noise of this place is not fun to be around.

If you want to see Coffs Harbour in all its glory, keep on driving past Reflections Holiday Park.

Lovely staff. Definitely dog friendly. Clean facilities. Central location. Cute rabbits running around.

FYI: You can hear the motorway from the cabins but depending on your tolerance it can be bearable.
Niice place to stay 😉🤗

Beautiful park with brand new, luxurious amenities.   Love the little wild rabbits running around!   Friendly staff.  Only minus was the highway noise but we bought earplugs so slept fine.  Overall very happy.

Noisy Traffic Noise but friendly staff.
Not Crammed Plenty Of Space Space Between Vans.
We had our bikes tampered with whilst on our motor home but otherwise a good stay.
Do Not Leave Clothes Out Overnight Even With Lighting I Had An Expensive  Pull Over Stolen From The Front Door Of My Motorhome Under The Awning.
The Park Has Thieve Visitors Or Local Thieves.
Be Aware & LOCK Everything Up.
Large roomy sites.
$35 a night atm

Nice people
Cool retro budget accommodation
Cabins have all you need if you're travelling light
Very close to town 👌
Stayed in own caravan.

Really lovely friendly hosts, made you feel at home which was lovely.
They are definately dog friendly and have poop bags around the park.
We had dinner at the camp kitchen and lit the fire on the lawn and there is a post with hooks to tie your doggies up too so super handy.
The only one problem we did have was traffic noise from the riad where we were. But you expect this due to the location.
They also have a free doggy bath to use with warm water.
Nice place. Friendly staff. Pity it rained for two days but that's nobodies fault.
